transcript.clean_text: game over one kilometer to go welcome to stage six of paranese 2021 the longest of this edition at 202.5 kilometers it's a day for baroda and breakaway specialists with five climbs between bruno and beyot there is also a sting in the tail a 2k climb to the finish line which is likely to put the pure sprinters out of contention plenty of king of the mountains points available today so no surprise to see anthony perez up at the front the frenchman tightening his grip on the polka dot jersey by cresting four of the five climbs in the lead a couple of former paranese stage winners also in this six-man breakaway alexey lutzenko and jonathan iver as well as kenny ellison julian alfares and victor kampernitz the world hour record holder their maximum lead was four minutes but jumbo vismo were keeping a close eye on things at the front of the peloton for primos roglich bad news though for brandon mcnulty sitting third overall and wearing the white jersey the young american had to abandon after a crash bike exchange during the chase on a day that looked good for michael matthews the gap down to two minutes with 60k still to go perez and alfares soon lost contact with the lead group leaving four men out at the front the kazakh champion lutsenko was then held up by a puncture and the tour de france stage winner was unable to catch his fellow escapees in the end elisande had the best legs in the break and the trek segafrodo man was still going solo with the second intermediate sprint 17.6k from the line he was then joined by another ef education nippo rider jonas ruch and they still had 20 seconds on the peloton heading to the business end of the stage all the different sprint teams trying to get organized while maximilian schachmann and alexander vlasov tacked back onto the back of the peloton after a couple of mechanical issues and a puncture rich going solo but he simply couldn't hold them off the german was caught just before the final kilometer a lot of the fast men also going backwards before that tough final hike to the finish line rockledge is getting ready to strike laporte is ready to go with him they round the final couple of twists and turns and roglitch is looking good for this victory roglic starts the sprint laporte is also there michael matthews on the wheel roglich is going for another stage victory matthew's trying to close in on the right-hand side on the line it is the yellow jersey he shouts as he crosses the line in front of me roglich wins another stage another remarkable display of power and panache from primos roglich to take his second win of this paranise and tighten his grip on the yellow and white jersey the 31 year old is looking on ominous form as he gears up for the classics including liege bastogne where he'll be defending his title roglich targeting a 50th career win on la comie and tomorrow my focus is on myself if uh if i can stay focused and uh yeah with the with the whole team that we do our job uh then it's fine and uh yeah then normally results also follow that roglic powering to the line ahead of christophe laporte michael matthews and dylan turns picking up another 10 bonus seconds a solid effort from sprinter brian cocker to come in seventh the slovenian champion lead shachmann by 41 seconds overall as jan isagire moves up to third place with mcnulty crashing out vlassoff is now the race's best young rider ahead of matteo jorgensen roglich also takes back the green jersey from sam bennett and it's not going to be easy for the irishman to recover it between now and sunday perez meanwhile on course to finish as the king of the mountains he boasts a commanding lead of 29 points over julian bernard we can expect to see both him and roglich on the move in tomorrow's queen stage where the jumbo vismo leader could all but secure overall victory you
